lines.en_lit: from the scent of water it will blossom and put forth branches like a plant”

And he would make a feast for his loved ones. The Sages taught There was an incident involving one High Priest who exited the Holy Temple and everyone followed him. When they saw Shemaya and Avtalyon, they left by himself and walked after Shemaya and Avtalyon.

Eventually, Shemaya and Avtalyon came to take leave of the High Priest he said to them: Let the descendants of nations come in peace. They said to him: Let the descendants of the nations come in peace, who perform the acts of Aaron, and let not a descendant of Aaron come in peace, who does not perform the acts of Aaron

MISHNA: the High Priest serves in eight garments, and the common priest in four: In a tunic and trousers and a mitre and a belt. The High Priest adds beyond A breastplate, and an ephod, and a robe, and a frontplate. in these be consulted for Urim VeTummim. And he may be consulted only on behalf of the king, or on behalf of the president of the court, or on behalf of one whom the community needs.
GEMARA: The Sages taught items it is stated linen [shesh], their threads six-fold, twined, eight fold. robe twelve curtain twenty-four breastplate and ephod twenty-eight

the threads six-fold, from where do we The verse states: “And they made the tunics of linen shesh And the mitre of linen, and the adorning mitres of linen, and the linen [bad] trousers of twined linen” Five mentions written; One for that itself, that they should be linen. And one that the threads should be six-fold, And one should be spun together And one to the other garments, shesh is not stated And one indispensable

From where be inferred that this shesh linen? Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, said: As the verse states: “Bad,” something which sprouts from the ground stalk by stalk [bad bad], And say wool, Wool splits, flax also splits Flax, splits when beaten.
Ravina said: from here: “They shall have linen pishtim mitres upon their heads, and linen trousers upon their loins”

Rav Ashi said to him: But before Ezekiel came, who said And according to your reasoning, that which Rav Ḥisda said This matter we did not learn from the Torah of Moses our teacher, we learned it from the words of Ezekiel ben Buzi, “No foreigner, uncircumcised of heart or uncircumcised of flesh, shall enter into My sanctuary” Before Ezekiel came, who said Rather, they learned it as a tradition and Ezekiel came and supported it with a verse. Here, too, they learned it as a tradition and Ezekiel came and supported it with a verse.

twined eight from where do we As it is written: “And they made upon the skirts of the robe pomegranates of sky-blue, and purple, and scarlet, twined” and derive “twined” “twined” from the curtain: Just as there, twenty-four so too here, twenty-four that each one of them was eight

Let us derive from breastplate and ephod Just as there, twenty-eight so too, here twenty-eight derive an item, with regard to which gold is not stated, from an item, with regard to which gold is not stated. to exclude breastplate and ephod, with regard to which gold is stated. On the contrary, derive garment from garment, to exclude curtain, which is a tent,

Rather, is derived from the belt, and a garment and an item with regard to which gold is not stated, are derived a garment and an item with regard to which gold is not stated. And an item with no gold, is not derived from an item that has gold in it,
Rav Mari said you shall make it” is written you shall make it, for this, and not for anything else.

Rav Ashi said “And you shall make is written that all the makings of it must be equal, how should we do Let us make three of ten each; they are thirty Let us make two of nine and one of ten; the verse states: “And you shall make,” that all the makings of it must be equal.
robe twelve from where do we As it is written: “And you shall make the robe of the ephod
